title: Fix atom-exhaustion-prone via: Registry tuple
prompt:
This GenServer uses `String.to_atom/1` to construct a dynamic process name for `start_link`:

```elixir
def start_link(user_id) do
  name = String.to_atom("session_#{user_id}")
  GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, user_id, name: name)
end
```

Each unique `user_id` creates a new permanent atom. Rewrite it so the process is registered through `Registry` with a string key (`{:via, Registry, {MyApp.SessionRegistry, user_id}}`). Do not call `String.to_atom/1`, `List.to_atom/1`, or use atom sigil interpolation anywhere. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/session_server.ex`. Also export a helper `name_for(user_id)` that returns the via-tuple, so callers don't reimplement it.
tier_rationale: Medium because the fix requires knowing the Registry via-tuple idiom, not just removing String.to_atom.
